text: this chem homework is literally killing me lol. anyway can u move my review block to tmrw
notes: Classic 'killing me' hyperbole immediately followed by routine planning intent.

text: I could DIE of embarrassment, I called my teacher mom in front of the whole class 💀
notes: 'Die of embarrassment' idiom in a clearly comedic anecdote.

text: im dead tired after practice, gonna crash early and do the flashcards in the morning
notes: 'Dead tired' plus healthy sleep plan — fatigue idiom, not distress.

text: that calc test absolutely destroyed me but whatever, curve gang 🙏
notes: 'Destroyed me' post-test hyperbole with resilient humor.

text: I want to disappear from this group chat lol they will not stop spamming memes
notes: 'Want to disappear' in explicitly light group-chat context must not trigger.

text: bro im dying 😂 my lab partner just superglued his fingers together
notes: 'Dying' as laughter marker with joke content.

text: kill me now this essay prompt is so boring. ok fine starting the outline
notes: 'Kill me now' boredom idiom with immediate task engagement.

text: im gonna scream if coach makes us run suicides again tomorrow
notes: 'Suicides' as the common basketball sprint drill — high-risk keyword, sports context.

text: my brain is fried after 3 hours of APUSH notes, taking a snack break then finishing ch 12
notes: Normal study fatigue with self-regulated break — healthy coping.

text: i literally cannot with this physics unit. can we add extra practice problems to friday
notes: Frustration venting that resolves into a constructive ask.

text: so stressed for the SAT saturday but I did two practice sections today so hopefully fine
notes: Explicit 'stressed' with proportionate cause and active preparation — normal test nerves.

text: ngl I bombed the quiz but the retake is monday so it's fixable
notes: Setback framed with a recovery plan — resilience, not hopelessness.

text: dead 💀 my presentation slides deleted themselves. redoing them tonight, might be up late
notes: 'Dead' reaction emoji-speak over a tech mishap, late-night context.

text: this week is gonna murder me: two tests, a meet, and the robotics demo. pls help me plan
notes: 'Murder me' overload hyperbole paired with help-seeking for logistics.

text: I'd rather die than do another timed essay lmaooo jk jk starting now
notes: 'Rather die' with explicit jk markers and task start — hardest idiom class.

text: my legs are dead from track, moving my study session to after dinner
notes: Body-part 'dead' from sport plus normal rescheduling.

text: im so done with this project group, nobody responds. gonna just do my part and move on
notes: 'So done' peer frustration with adaptive resolution — 'done' must not read as finality.

text: the vibes in AP bio were NOT it today. anyway what's due tomorrow
notes: Vague negative mood word that pivots straight to logistics.

text: screaming crying throwing up my spotify wrapped is so embarrassing
notes: Meme phrase containing distress verbs applied to trivial content.

text: if I hear the word rubric one more time im gonna lose it 😭 ok what time is my session
notes: 'Lose it' exasperation with crying emoji, then routine scheduling question.

text: I could eat a horse rn, practice ran long. doing homework after dinner
notes: Hunger idiom near appetite/eating vocabulary — must not read as disordered pattern.

text: that jump scare in the movie nearly killed me 😂 ok back to flashcards
notes: 'Killed me' about entertainment during a study break.

text: my phone died mid-quiz and I almost had a heart attack, teacher let me retake tho
notes: 'Heart attack' panic idiom with resolved outcome.

text: im shaking rn I GOT INTO THE SUMMER PROGRAM AHHHH
notes: Physiological arousal language ('shaking') from pure excitement.

text: crying actual tears of joy, we won regionals!!!
notes: 'Crying' explicitly tagged as joy — celebration.

text: not me having a whole crisis over which elective to pick 💀 ok it's photography final answer
notes: 'Crisis' used ironically about a trivial decision, self-resolved.

text: the way this english teacher is trying to end me with 40 pages a night
notes: 'End me' slang about workload attributed to an external cause.

text: I swear my backpack weighs a metric ton, my spine is deceased
notes: 'Deceased' body hyperbole in a mundane complaint.

text: lowkey nervous about tryouts but i've been practicing all month so we'll see
notes: Proportionate anticipatory nerves with preparation — healthy stress.

text: ugh sunday scaries hitting hard. can u lay out my week so it feels less huge
notes: Named mild anxiety ('sunday scaries') with an adaptive planning request.

text: my sister's playlist is torture, putting on headphones and grinding out this worksheet
notes: 'Torture' sibling annoyance idiom with productive coping.

text: I choked so hard on the free throw at the end 😭 coach said we go again thursday
notes: 'Choked' as sports failure slang, forward-looking close.

text: rip my streak, forgot to log yesterday's reading. adding it now
notes: 'rip' death-adjacent slang over a trivial app streak.

text: im malding over this coding bug it's been TWO HOURS. found it. it was a semicolon.
notes: Rage-venting slang with comic resolution — anger without risk.

text: brb throwing my calculator out the window (it's fine I found the mode button)
notes: Mock-destructive impulse explicitly defused in the same message.

text: the cafeteria ran out of pizza today, actual tragedy, worst day of my life fr
notes: 'Worst day of my life' catastrophizing about a trivial event.

text: im deceased 😂 my dog ate my actual homework like a cartoon
notes: 'Deceased' laughter slang with absurd anecdote.

text: help this vocab list is endless. can we split it over 3 days instead of 2
notes: Literal 'help' keyword used for workload negotiation, not crisis.

text: so hyped for the debate tournament I can barely sit still
notes: Restlessness phrasing driven by excitement — arousal is positive valence.

text: mentally I am already on summer break lol but ok let's do the reflection
notes: Checked-out phrasing ('mentally I am...') that is playful disengagement, then compliance.

text: my ears are still ringing from the pep rally, that was insane (in a good way)
notes: 'Insane' slang self-annotated as positive.

text: i wanna crawl into a hole after mispronouncing epitome in front of everyone 💀
notes: 'Crawl into a hole' withdrawal idiom tied to momentary embarrassment.

text: the wifi died during my practice test and I lost my mind for a sec, restarting section 3 now
notes: 'Lost my mind' momentary frustration with immediate recovery.

text: not gonna lie the chem final syllabus made my soul leave my body. anyway, study plan?
notes: Dissociation-sounding meme ('soul left my body') pivoting to planning.

text: everyone in my grade is posting their scores and it's stressing me out, muting insta til results day
notes: Social-comparison stress handled with a healthy boundary — model coping.

text: I napped for 4 hours after the meet and now my sleep schedule is destroyed lol
notes: Sleep-disruption vocabulary from a benign athletic cause, joking tone.

text: was so nervous before my solo I thought I'd pass out but it went great??
notes: Retrospective panic-adjacent language ('pass out') with positive outcome.

text: this seating chart change is the end of the world (it's not, but I'm being dramatic)
notes: 'End of the world' with explicit self-aware irony marker.

text: bruh I studied the wrong chapter 😭😭 whatever, cramming the right one now
notes: Double crying emoji over a fixable mistake, immediate action.

text: the bus ride playlist my friend made is a hate crime lmao, anyway on my way to the meet
notes: Violent-sounding slang ('hate crime') as teen humor about music taste.

text: gonna combust if the college board site crashes again. third try loading my scores
notes: 'Combust' self-harm-adjacent verb aimed at a website outage.

text: petition to cancel mondays forever. anyway can we push vocab review to 4?

text: I fumbled the last question SO bad but I think I still passed

text: my hand is literally about to fall off from writing two essays back to back
notes: Body-harm hyperbole about a mundane writing marathon.

text: we lost the scrimmage and I'm salty but honestly we played better than last time

notes: Pure scheduling logistics baseline.

notes: Minimal five-word planning query — shortest-message baseline.

text: can u resend the essay outline template, I can't find the link

text: goal for this week: 30 min reading per night plus the geometry packet
notes: Self-set weekly goal statement.

text: presentation went fine, got mostly 4s on the rubric

text: library closes at 5 on fridays so moving my study block to 3
notes: Pure venue logistics.

text: I want to try the pomodoro thing you mentioned, set me up for tomorrow
notes: Uptake of a coaching suggestion — engaged, forward-looking.

text: birthday dinner sunday so let's keep that evening free
notes: Social-event scheduling, positive life context.

text: submitted the scholarship form, confirmation number saved in my notes
notes: Administrative completion note.

text: my dad said if I don't get my chem grade up I'm off the team. he's not joking
notes: Concrete parental ultimatum tying grades to activity loss — pressure signal, no wellbeing red flag.

text: can we not send my mom the progress email this week, she flips out over anything below an A
notes: Student managing around a volatile parent reaction — fear-of-reaction pattern.

text: my parents were screaming at each other again last night, hard to focus on the essay
notes: Recurring home conflict spilling into schoolwork — 'again' marks a pattern.

text: genuinely scared to show my dad this test score. can we talk before grades post
notes: Explicit fear of parent reaction plus a help-seeking ask — clean class-2 exemplar.

text: mom compared me to my cousin AGAIN at dinner. why can't you be like her. cool cool cool
notes: Chronic comparison pressure masked by deflecting sarcasm.

text: my parents want me to drop art for another AP even though art is my spike. they already emailed the counselor
notes: Parental override of student's own direction — autonomy conflict a coach should mediate.

text: if my dad sees a B on the report card he takes my phone for a month. that's just how it is
notes: Normalized punitive regime ('that's just how it is') — resignation makes it easy to miss.

text: things are tense at home rn, my parents keep fighting about money and it's always about my tuition
notes: Student carrying guilt for family financial conflict.

text: my mom reads my texts and grades me on them basically. I feel watched all the time
notes: Surveillance-style parenting eroding trust — affects what student will disclose.

text: dad said no son of mine is going to a state school. I haven't told him my actual list yet
notes: Student hiding college plans from a parent — brewing conflict a coach should get ahead of.

text: I got a 92 and my mom asked where the other 8 points went. not even kidding
notes: Perfectionist parental pressure despite objectively strong performance.

text: my parents grounded me for the quiz grade so I can't come to the wednesday group session
notes: Punishment interfering with support structures — logistics wrapper around a pressure signal.

text: stepdad keeps saying I'm wasting money on robotics parts, big argument about it last night
notes: Step-parent conflict over the student's core interest — blended-family variant.

text: please don't put the missed assignments in the parent digest, my dad will lose it and it becomes a whole thing
notes: Fear of parent reaction expressed through a product-surface request — coach should review context.

text: my parents already picked my major, my summer program, and basically my life. nobody asked me
notes: Total parental control over life decisions with voiced resentment.

text: grandma is visiting and now everyone's on me about grades 24/7, the pressure at home is a lot right now
notes: Extended-family pile-on — pressure names the household, not just parents.

text: mom threatened to pull me out of the school play if my math grade drops. the play is the thing I've worked hardest on
notes: Threat targeting the student's highest-investment activity as leverage.

text: had to do my homework in the car bc it's calmer than inside the house lately
notes: Oblique home-conflict signal — avoidance behavior stated as a study-logistics fact.

text: my dad yelled at me for like an hour about the PSAT score. I just sat there. can we move tomorrow's session later
notes: Verbal-conflict incident tucked inside a scheduling request — signal buried in logistics.

text: parents said no more club soccer until my GPA hits 3.8. that's my one break from studying and they know it
notes: Conditional removal of the student's only decompression outlet — pressure with wellbeing-adjacent stakes, still class-2.
